71.title: New present class: krenem, dođem
71.intro: A new family of verbs joins your present tense today, and they all take the regular -em endings. Verbs in -nuti conjugate with -nem (krenuti → krenem), and doći with its whole family gives -đem (dođem, nađem, uđem). These are the key motion and event verbs of everyday plans — learn the 1st person singular and the rest of the paradigm follows automatically.

items.en: to set off → I set off
items.note: Full paradigm: krenem, kreneš, krene, krenemo, krenete, krenu. Kad krenemo ranije, ne žurimo. (When we set off earlier, we don't hurry.)

items.en: to come, to arrive → I come
items.note: dođem, dođeš, dođe, dođemo, dođete, dođu. Dođem po tebe u osam. (I'll come pick you up at eight.)

items.en: to arrive, to make it → I arrive
items.note: stignem, stigneš… stignu. Javi mi se kad stigneš. (Let me know when you arrive.)

items.en: to set out → I set out
items.note: pođem, pođeš… pođu. Ako pođemo sada, stižemo na vrijeme. (If we set out now, we arrive on time.)

items.en: to leave, to go away → I leave
items.note: Irregular stem: odem, odeš, ode, odemo, odete, odu. Moram otići prije šest. (I have to leave before six.)

items.en: to go out → I go out
items.note: izađem… izađu. Kad izađem s posla, nazvat ću te. (When I get out of work, I'll call you.)

items.en: to enter → I enter
items.note: uđem… uđu. Imperative: Uđi, vrata su otvorena! (Come in, the door is open!)

items.en: to find → I find
items.note: nađem… nađu. With se it means to meet up: Nađemo se u pet? (Shall we meet at five?) — the perfective present is normal in arrangements.

items.en: to turn (left/right) → I turn
items.note: skrenem… skrenu. Skreni desno kod pekare. (Turn right at the bakery.)

items.en: to take off; to download → I take off
items.note: skinem… skinu. Skinem jaknu čim uđem u stan. (I take my jacket off as soon as I enter the flat.)

items.en: to lift, to raise → I lift
items.note: dignem… dignu. With se, colloquially 'get up': Ujutro se teško dignem iz kreveta.

items.en: to begin → I begin
items.note: Not a -nuti verb, but the same -nem pattern: počnem, počneš… počnu. Čim počne kiša, idemo unutra. (As soon as the rain starts, we go inside.)

71.title: krenem, dođem, stignem in action
71.intro: Conjugate the new class and keep your clitic order intact while you do it.

questions.prompt: What is the present form of 'doći' for 'oni' (they)?
questions.explanation: doći follows the -em class: dođem, dođeš, dođe, dođemo, dođete, dođu.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'stići' (ti): Javi mi se kad ______ kući.
questions.explanation: stići → stignem, stigneš… Javi mi se kad stigneš kući. (Let me know when you get home.)

questions.prompt: Choose the correct present form of 'krenuti' for 'mi' (we):
questions.explanation: krenuti → krenem, so 'mi' is krenemo. (krenimo is the imperative 'let's set off'.)

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'otići' (ona): Ona uvijek ______ prva s posla.
questions.explanation: otići has an irregular present stem: odem, odeš, ode… Ona uvijek ode prva. (She always leaves first.)

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Shall we meet in front of the cinema at eight?'
questions.answer: Nađemo se ispred kina u osam
questions.explanation: Nađemo se… is the standard way to propose meeting up; the clitic se sits in second position.

questions.prompt: Which sentence has correct clitic order? (I'll bring it to him tomorrow.)
questions.explanation: The clitic chain in second position: auxiliary (ću) → dative (mu) → accusative (ga).

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'doći' (vi): Kad ______ k nama?
questions.explanation: doći → dođem, and for 'vi' it is dođete: Kad dođete k nama? (When are you coming to our place?)

questions.prompt: Čim uđem u stan, ______ jaknu.
questions.explanation: skinuti → skinem (ja). As soon as I enter the flat, I take off my jacket.

72.title: Invitations & arrangements: Dođi k nama!
72.intro: Arranging to meet is a core A2 skill, and a handful of fixed patterns covers the whole social ritual: inviting, proposing a time, accepting, and declining politely with a reason. Notice how pronoun clitics live inside almost every phrase (Javi mi se! Odgovara li ti…?).

items.en: Come to our place on Saturday!
items.note: k + dative = to someone's place. Formal/plural: Dođite k nama!

items.hr: Hoćemo li se naći u petak navečer?
items.en: Shall we meet on Friday evening?
items.note: li right after the verb, then the clitic se — the standard question for arranging.

items.en: Does Sunday work for you?
items.note: odgovarati + dative: the day 'answers to' you. Formal: Odgovara li Vam…?

items.en: When suits you?
items.note: The open version of the same question.

items.en: Sure! / That works!
items.note: The universal casual yes to any proposal.

items.en: Deal! / Agreed!
items.note: Seals an arrangement: Vidimo se u pet. — Vrijedi!

items.en: I'll gladly come.
items.note: rado = gladly; a warm way to accept.

items.en: I'm sorry, I can't.
items.note: The polite refusal opener — always soften with žao mi je.

items.hr: Ne mogu jer imam već dogovor.
items.en: I can't because I already have an arrangement.
items.note: Decline + jer + reason. dogovor = an arrangement/appointment.

items.en: Maybe another time.
items.note: Keeps the door open after declining.

items.en: Get in touch with me!
items.note: javiti se + dative. The clitics mi se follow the imperative directly.

items.en: See you!
items.note: Literally 'we see each other' — the default goodbye between friends.

72.title: Invite, accept, decline
72.intro: Run the whole invitation ritual: propose, check the time, say yes, say no politely — with every clitic in its place.

questions.prompt: Your friend asks: 'Hoćemo li se naći u petak?' How do you casually accept?
questions.explanation: Može! (literally 'it can') is the universal casual yes. Vrijedi! works too.

questions.prompt: Complete the invitation: Dođi ___ nama u subotu!
questions.explanation: k + dative = to someone's place: Dođi k nama! (Come to our place!)

questions.prompt: Which is the polite way to decline an invitation?
options: Žao mi je, ne mogu, imam već dogovor.
questions.answer: Žao mi je, ne mogu, imam već dogovor.
questions.explanation: Soften with žao mi je (I'm sorry), then give a reason — the standard polite refusal.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Shall we meet on Friday?'
questions.answer: Hoćemo li se naći u petak
questions.explanation: Verb first, then the clitics in fixed order: li before se — Hoćemo li se naći…?

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing clitic: Javi ___ se sutra!
questions.explanation: javiti se + dative: Javi mi se! = Get in touch with me! Dative clitic (mi) comes before se.

questions.prompt: Asking a friend if Saturday works: Odgovara li ___ subota?
questions.explanation: odgovarati takes the dative: Odgovara li ti subota? (Does Saturday suit you?)

questions.prompt: Decline with a reason: Ne mogu doći ______ imam previše posla.
questions.explanation: jer = because. Ne mogu doći jer imam previše posla. (I can't come because I have too much work.)

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Let's meet in front of the café at five.'
questions.answer: Nađimo se ispred kafića u pet
questions.explanation: Nađimo se = let's meet (imperative of naći se); ispred + genitive (kafića).

72.title: Roštilj u subotu — sending the invitations
72.intro: You and your wife are planning a Saturday barbecue and inviting friends. Read your lines ('Me') aloud — one friend accepts, one declines, and every phrase is from today's lesson.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dušo, hoćemo li pozvati Anu i Marka na roštilj u subotu?
lines.en: Honey, shall we invite Ana and Marko to a barbecue on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Može! Ja ću im poslati poruku. U koliko sati?
lines.en: Sure! I'll send them a message. At what time?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oko pet. Odgovara li ti to?
lines.en: Around five. Does that work for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Odgovara. Napisao sam im: 'Dođite k nama u subotu oko pet!'
lines.en: It works. I wrote to them: 'Come to our place on Saturday around five!'

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Super. Što je Ana odgovorila?
lines.en: Great. What did Ana reply?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ana je napisala: 'Rado ću doći! Vidimo se u subotu!'
lines.en: Ana wrote: 'I'll gladly come! See you on Saturday!'

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično! A Marko? Može li on?
lines.en: Excellent! And Marko? Can he make it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Marko ne može. Žao mu je, ali već ima dogovor.
lines.en: Marko can't. He's sorry, but he already has an arrangement.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Šteta. Onda ćemo ga pozvati drugi put.
lines.en: A pity. Then we'll invite him another time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vrijedi. Javit ću mu se sljedeći tjedan.
lines.en: Deal. I'll get in touch with him next week.

73.title: Building a weekend-plan message
73.intro: Today you write a real message arranging the weekend. These are the building blocks natives use — greeting, plan, time, practical details, and sign-off. Watch the enclitics: in writing, every ću/mi/se must sit in second position, and a message is the perfect place to check each one deliberately.

items.en: Hi! What are you doing on Saturday?
items.note: The standard casual opener for making plans.

items.hr: Planiramo roštilj kod nas u dvorištu.
items.en: We're planning a barbecue at our place in the yard.
items.note: kod nas = at our place.

items.en: We start around five in the afternoon.
items.note: oko + genitive for approximate time: oko pet(a sata).

items.hr: Bit će i Ana i njezin muž.
items.en: Ana and her husband will be there too.
items.note: Bit će = futur I of biti; i… i… = both… and…

items.hr: Ponesi nešto za piće ako želiš.
items.en: Bring something to drink if you like.
items.note: ponijeti → imperative ponesi.

items.en: If it rains, we stay inside.
items.note: A simple real condition with ako + present.

items.en: Let me know whether you can come.
items.note: Indirect question with li: možeš li = whether you can.

items.hr: Možemo se naći i ranije ako ti odgovara.
items.en: We can also meet earlier if that suits you.
items.note: se in second position of its clause: Možemo se naći…

items.en: I'm looking forward to it!
items.note: veseliti se — the reflexive se is obligatory.

items.en: Say hi to Ivana!
items.note: pozdraviti + accusative — a warm closing touch.

items.en: See you on Saturday!
items.note: The classic sign-off for a plan message.

items.hr: P. S. Adresu ću ti poslati sutra.
items.en: P.S. I'll send you the address tomorrow.
items.note: Fronting Adresu pushes the clitics ću ti into second position — classic Croatian word order.

73.title: Every clitic in second position
73.intro: A written message is a clitic exam. Place ću, ti, se and li exactly where they belong.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I'll get in touch with you tomorrow.'
questions.explanation: Second position chain: auxiliary (ću) → dative (ti) → se. Javit ću ti se sutra.

questions.prompt: Which sentence has correct clitic order?
questions.explanation: After the first stressed word (Adresu), the clitics follow in order: ću (aux) → ti (dative).

questions.prompt: Complete the indirect question: Javi mi možeš ___ doći.
questions.explanation: možeš li = whether you can. li always attaches right after the verb.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Will you send me the address?'
questions.explanation: Question chain: verb + li + dative clitic (mi), then the infinitive and object.

questions.prompt: 'I'm looking forward to it!' = Veselim ___!
questions.explanation: veseliti se is reflexive — the se can never be dropped: Veselim se!

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing clitic: Naći ćemo ___ ispred ulaza.
questions.explanation: naći se = to meet up. The chain is ćemo (aux) → se: Naći ćemo se ispred ulaza.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct?
options: Sutra ćemo se naći u parku.
Sutra se ćemo naći u parku.
Ćemo se sutra naći u parku.
Sutra ćemo naći se u parku.
questions.answer: Sutra ćemo se naći u parku.
questions.explanation: Clitics in second position, auxiliary before se: Sutra ćemo se naći…

questions.prompt: Put the words in order (talking about 'broj' — the number): 'I'll send it to you tomorrow.'
questions.explanation: Full chain in action: ću (aux) → ti (dative) → ga (accusative). Poslat ću ti ga sutra.

74.title: Week 10 in ten lines
74.intro: Everything from this week compressed into ten model sentences: weekend plans in futur I, object pronouns in both forms, and the clitic chain that holds Croatian sentences together. If you can produce all ten cold, week 10 is yours.

items.hr: Ići ću na izlet u subotu.
items.en: I'll go on a trip on Saturday.
items.note: Futur I: infinitive + ću. Fronted verb form: Ići ću…

items.en: We'll meet at five.
items.note: ćemo → se: auxiliary before the reflexive clitic.

items.en: me, you, him, her — stressed/clitic accusative pairs
items.note: The short (clitic) forms are what natives use 95% of the time; also: nas, vas, njih/ih.

items.en: Do you see me? — I see you.
items.note: Clitic pronouns in the most common exchange there is.

items.hr: Poznaješ li Marka? — Da, poznajem ga.
items.en: Do you know Marko? — Yes, I know him.
items.note: Replace the noun with the clitic pronoun in the answer.

items.en: This is for you.
items.note: After prepositions you must use the stressed form: za tebe, never za te.

items.en: I gave it to him yesterday.
items.note: The full chain: sam (aux) → mu (dative) → ga (accusative).

items.en: She gave me the book.
items.note: je (the 3rd person auxiliary) always goes last in the chain: mi → je.

items.hr: Krenut ćemo rano, a stići ćemo oko podneva.
items.en: We'll set off early and arrive around noon.
items.note: This week's new verbs (krenuti, stići) in futur I.

items.hr: Javit ću ti se kad stignem.
items.en: I'll get in touch with you when I arrive.
items.note: ću → ti → se, then the perfective present (stignem) in the kad-clause.

74.title: Week 10 checkpoint — with listening
74.intro: A mixed review of futur I, object pronouns and clitic order — including three listening tasks. Play each audio and catch the essentials, just like you would in a real voice message.

questions.prompt: Listen: what is the plan?
options: A trip to Samobor on Saturday at nine
A movie on Friday at nine
questions.answer: A trip to Samobor on Saturday at nine
questions.explanation: U subotu idemo na izlet u Samobor. Krećemo u devet ujutro. = On Saturday we're going on a trip to Samobor. We set off at nine in the morning.
questions.audioText: U subotu idemo na izlet u Samobor. Krećemo u devet ujutro.

questions.prompt: Listen: what does the speaker propose?
options: Meeting on Sunday instead of Friday
questions.answer: Meeting on Sunday instead of Friday
questions.explanation: Žao mi je, ne mogu doći u petak. Imam već dogovor. Može li u nedjelju? = Sorry, I can't come on Friday — I already have an arrangement. How about Sunday?
questions.audioText: Žao mi je, ne mogu doći u petak. Imam već dogovor. Može li u nedjelju?

questions.prompt: Listen and type the missing clitic: Javit ću ___ se kad stignem.
questions.explanation: Javit ću ti se kad stignem. — the dative clitic ti comes between ću and se.
questions.audioText: Javit ću ti se kad stignem.

questions.prompt: Vidiš li Anu? — Da, vidim ___.
questions.explanation: The accusative clitic for 'her' is je: Vidim je. (Next to the auxiliary je it switches to ju: Vidio ju je.)

questions.prompt: Replace the noun with a pronoun: Poznaješ li Marka? — Da, poznajem ___.
questions.explanation: njega/ga — the clitic ga stands for Marka: Poznajem ga. (I know him.)

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I gave it to him yesterday.'
questions.explanation: Second position after Jučer: sam (aux) → mu (dative) → ga (accusative), then the participle.

questions.prompt: Što ćeš raditi u subotu? — ______ na roštilj kod prijatelja.
questions.explanation: ću can't stand first, so the infinitive fronts it: Ići ću na roštilj. (I'll go to a barbecue.)

questions.prompt: How do you say 'Can you send it to me?' (it = paket)
questions.explanation: The chain: li → dative (mi) → accusative (ga). Možeš li mi ga poslati?

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition: Ovo je ___ tebe.
questions.explanation: za tebe = for you. After a preposition the stressed form (tebe) is obligatory.

74.title: Planning the real weekend
74.intro: Saturday is coming and this plan is actually happening. Negotiate the whole weekend with your wife — market, barbecue at Ana's, and a quiet Sunday — using this week's futur I, pronouns and clitics.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dušo, kakav nam je plan za vikend?
lines.en: Honey, what's our plan for the weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: U subotu ujutro idemo na plac, a poslije možemo na kavu.
lines.en: On Saturday morning we're going to the market, and afterwards we can go for coffee.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Može. A poslijepodne? Ana nas je pozvala na roštilj.
lines.en: Sure. And in the afternoon? Ana invited us to a barbecue.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Znam, odgovorio sam joj jučer. Rekao sam da dolazimo oko pet.
lines.en: I know, I replied to her yesterday. I said we're coming around five.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično. Hoćemo li joj nešto ponijeti?
lines.en: Excellent. Shall we bring her something?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ponijet ćemo vino. Kupit ću ga u subotu ujutro na placu.
lines.en: We'll bring wine. I'll buy it on Saturday morning at the market.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Savršeno. A u nedjelju? Ja se želim odmoriti.
lines.en: Perfect. And on Sunday? I want to rest.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Naravno. Ujutro ćemo dugo spavati, a onda idemo u šetnju uz rijeku.
lines.en: Of course. In the morning we'll sleep in, and then we're going for a walk along the river.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Baš lijepo. Kad krećemo u subotu?
lines.en: How nice. When do we set off on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Krenut ćemo u devet. Javit ću ti se s placa ako nešto trebamo.
lines.en: We'll set off at nine. I'll get in touch with you from the market if we need anything.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dogovoreno. Veselim se vikendu!
lines.en: Agreed. I'm looking forward to the weekend!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: I ja. Napokon slobodni dani!
lines.en: Me too. Free days at last!

75.title: Kvaliteta života — home, neighbourhood, habits
75.intro: Quality-of-life vocabulary powers the conversations natives most like to have: how people live, what their neighbourhood is like, which habits they love. These fourteen words are the core kit — each comes with a sentence you can reuse about your own life.

items.en: flat, apartment
items.note: Živim u stanu u centru. (I live in a flat in the centre.)

items.en: neighbourhood
items.note: Naš kvart je miran i zelen. (Our neighbourhood is quiet and green.)

items.en: neighbour (m/f)
items.note: Naši susjedi su jako ljubazni. (Our neighbours are very kind.)

items.en: building
items.note: Stanujemo u staroj zgradi bez lifta. (We live in an old building without a lift.)

items.en: yard, courtyard
items.note: Djeca se igraju u dvorištu. (The children play in the yard.)

items.en: habit
items.note: Jutarnja kava je moja najdraža navika. (Morning coffee is my favourite habit.)

items.en: rest; vacation
items.note: Trebam odmor od posla. (I need a rest from work.)

items.en: noise
items.note: Ne mogu spavati od buke. (I can't sleep because of the noise.)

items.en: peace, quiet
items.note: Navečer je u kvartu potpuni mir. (In the evening the neighbourhood is completely quiet.)

items.en: nature
items.note: Vikendom volimo biti u prirodi. (On weekends we love being in nature.)

items.en: a walk
items.note: Idemo u šetnju poslije večere. (We go for a walk after dinner.)

items.en: free time
items.note: U slobodno vrijeme čitam i kuham. (In my free time I read and cook.)

items.en: costs, expenses
items.note: Troškovi života u centru su visoki. (The cost of living in the centre is high.)

items.en: closeness, vicinity
items.note: U blizini je park i tržnica. (There's a park and a market nearby.)

75.title: How you live: vocabulary in context
75.intro: Use the new lifestyle words in real sentences about homes, habits and neighbourhoods.

questions.prompt: Naš ______ je jako tih — nema puno prometa.
questions.explanation: kvart = neighbourhood. Naš kvart je jako tih. (Our neighbourhood is very quiet.)

questions.prompt: Complete (buka, after 'od'): Ne mogu spavati od ______ .
questions.explanation: od + genitive: buka → buke. Ne mogu spavati od buke. (I can't sleep because of the noise.)

questions.prompt: What does 'susjed' mean?
questions.explanation: susjed = neighbour (male); susjeda = female neighbour.

questions.prompt: Complete: Svako jutro pijem kavu na balkonu — to je moja ______ .
questions.explanation: navika = habit. A daily routine you repeat is your navika.

questions.prompt: U slobodno vrijeme volim ______ u prirodi.
questions.explanation: After voljeti you need the infinitive: volim šetati (I love walking). šetnja is the noun 'a walk'.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I live in a quiet neighbourhood near the park.'
questions.answer: Živim u mirnom kvartu blizu parka
questions.explanation: u + locative (mirnom kvartu), blizu + genitive (parka).

questions.prompt: U centru je uvijek buka, a u našem kvartu je ______ .
questions.explanation: The classic contrast: buka (noise) vs mir (peace and quiet).

questions.prompt: Complete (blizina, after 'u'): Volim svoj stan jer je sve u ______ .
questions.explanation: u blizini = nearby (locative of blizina). Sve je u blizini. (Everything is close by.)

questions.prompt: Želim promijeniti jednu ______ : previše gledam mobitel.
questions.explanation: Direct object of promijeniti → accusative: navika → naviku.

76.title: Locative across the whole phrase
76.intro: You already put nouns in the locative; now the whole phrase agrees. Masculine and neuter modifiers take -om(e) (u tom velikom gradu), soft stems take -em (na trećem katu, u našem dvorištu), and feminine takes -oj (u našoj novoj kući). Bonus: these adjective endings are identical in the dative, so you're learning two cases at once.

items.hr: taj veliki grad → u tom velikom gradu
items.en: that big city → in that big city
items.note: Masculine locative: both modifiers take -om.

items.hr: moj mali stan → u mom malom stanu
items.en: my small flat → in my small flat
items.note: moj → mom (also mome/mojem). Živim u mom malom stanu.

items.hr: naša nova kuća → u našoj novoj kući
items.en: our new house → in our new house
items.note: Feminine: modifiers take -oj, the noun takes -i.

items.en: one's own room → in one's own room
items.note: svoj declines like moj: Radim u svojoj sobi. (I work in my own room.)

items.hr: ovaj tihi kvart → u ovom tihom kvartu
items.en: this quiet neighbourhood → in this quiet neighbourhood
items.note: ovaj → ovom, tihi → tihom.

items.hr: to lijepo selo → u tom lijepom selu
items.en: that pretty village → in that pretty village
items.note: Neuter takes the same -om endings as masculine.

items.en: third floor → on the third floor
items.note: Soft stem ć → -em: trećem, not trećom. Stan je na trećem katu.

items.hr: naše veliko dvorište → u našem velikom dvorištu
items.en: our big yard → in our big yard
items.note: naš is a soft stem too: našem.

items.hr: jedna mala kuhinja → u jednoj maloj kuhinji
items.en: one small kitchen → in one small kitchen
items.note: Feminine chain: jednoj maloj kuhinji.

items.hr: tvoj stari kvart → u tvom starom kvartu
items.en: your old neighbourhood → in your old neighbourhood
items.note: tvoj → tvom, stari → starom.

items.en: the main square → on the main square
items.note: Squares and open spaces take na: na glavnom trgu.

76.title: u tom velikom gradu — full phrase practice
76.intro: Decline whole phrases in the locative and keep the accusative-vs-locative contrast (motion vs location) sharp.

questions.prompt: Complete with the locative of 'moj': Živim u ______ malom stanu.
questions.explanation: moj → mom (mome/mojem) in the locative: u mom malom stanu.

questions.prompt: Radim u ______ velikom gradu. (taj)
questions.explanation: taj → tom in the locative: u tom velikom gradu.

questions.prompt: Complete with the locative of 'svoj': Radim u ______ sobi.
questions.explanation: Feminine locative -oj: u svojoj sobi = in my own room (the owner is the subject, so svoj).

questions.prompt: Stan je na ______ katu.
questions.explanation: treći has a soft stem (ć), so the locative is trećem: na trećem katu.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I live in this quiet neighbourhood.'
questions.explanation: The whole phrase agrees in the locative: ovom tihom kvartu.

questions.prompt: Motion or location? Svaki dan idem ______ .
questions.explanation: ići expresses motion → accusative: idem u veliki grad. Location (living there) would be locative.

questions.prompt: Motion or location? Cijeli život živim ______ .
questions.explanation: živjeti expresses location → locative: živim u velikom gradu.

questions.prompt: Complete with the locative of 'naš': Odmaramo se u ______ novoj kući.
questions.explanation: Feminine locative -oj: u našoj novoj kući.

questions.prompt: Baka živi ______ .
questions.explanation: selo is neuter and takes the same locative endings as masculine: u tom lijepom selu.

77.title: Talking about where you live
77.intro: Time to describe your home like a native: which flat, which floor, what you love and what bothers you. Notice two key constructions — sviđati se and smetati both put YOU in the dative (sviđa mi se, smeta mi) while the thing itself is the subject.

items.en: I live in a two-room flat.
items.note: dvosoban = two-room; jednosoban, trosoban follow the same pattern.

items.hr: Stan je na trećem katu, u mirnoj zgradi.
items.en: The flat is on the third floor, in a quiet building.
items.note: Yesterday's locative phrases doing real work.

items.hr: Imamo velik balkon s pogledom na park.
items.en: We have a big balcony with a view of the park.
items.note: s pogledom na + accusative = with a view of.

items.hr: U mom kvartu ima puno kafića i mala tržnica.
items.en: In my neighbourhood there are lots of cafés and a small market.
items.note: ima = there is/are.

items.en: I love my own study most of all.
items.note: svoju because the owner (I) is the subject; radna soba = study/workroom.

items.hr: Sviđa mi se što je sve blizu.
items.en: I like that everything is close.
items.note: sviđati se + dative: the liker is mi, the liked thing is the subject.

items.en: The noise from the street bothers me.
items.note: smetati works the same way: smeta mi = it bothers me.

items.hr: Odrastao sam u maloj kući s dvorištem.
items.en: I grew up in a small house with a yard.
items.note: odrasti → odrastao sam (masculine participle); s + instrumental: s dvorištem.

items.hr: Prije sam živio u kući, a sada živim u stanu.
items.en: Before, I lived in a house, and now I live in a flat.
items.note: The then-vs-now frame you'll reuse constantly.

items.en: What kind of flat do you live in?
items.note: kakav declines too: u kakvom stanu (locative).

items.hr: Što ti se sviđa u svom kvartu?
items.en: What do you like about your neighbourhood?
items.note: The question that starts every good conversation about how people live.

77.title: Describe your home
77.intro: Practise the home-description toolkit: dative constructions with sviđati se and smetati, plus locative phrases about rooms and floors.

questions.prompt: Sviđa ___ se tvoj stan. (I like your flat.)
questions.explanation: sviđati se puts the liker in the dative: Sviđa mi se tvoj stan.

questions.prompt: Complete with the locative of 'kakav': U ______ stanu živiš?
questions.explanation: kakav → kakvom in the locative: U kakvom stanu živiš? (What kind of flat do you live in?)

questions.prompt: Smeta mi ______ s ulice.
questions.explanation: With smetati, the annoying thing is the SUBJECT (nominative buka); the person is dative (mi).

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I love my own study most of all.'
questions.explanation: svoju (not moju) because the owner is the subject; the whole phrase agrees in the accusative.

questions.prompt: Complete with the locative of 'kuća': Odrastao sam u maloj ______ .
questions.explanation: Feminine locative: kuća → kući. Odrastao sam u maloj kući. (I grew up in a small house.)

questions.prompt: Što ti se sviđa u ______ kvartu?
questions.explanation: svoj → svom in the locative: u svom kvartu (in your own neighbourhood — the owner is the subject 'you').

questions.prompt: Imamo velik balkon s ______ na park.
questions.explanation: s + instrumental: s pogledom na park = with a view of the park.

questions.prompt: Complete with the locative of 'dvosoban': Živim u ______ stanu.
questions.explanation: dvosoban → dvosobnom (locative, with fleeting a dropped): u dvosobnom stanu.

77.title: Naš stan, naš kvart — a home conversation
77.intro: An evening chat with your wife about your flat, your neighbourhood, and where each of you grew up. Her lines use feminine past forms (odrasla, živjela) — a perfect model of participle agreement.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Znaš, jako volim naš stan. A ti?
lines.en: You know, I really love our flat. And you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: I ja. Najviše volim našu dnevnu sobu jer je svijetla.
lines.en: Me too. I love our living room most because it's bright.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meni se najviše sviđa kuhinja. U njoj pijemo jutarnju kavu.
lines.en: I like the kitchen most. We drink our morning coffee in it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Istina. A sviđa mi se i kvart — sve je u blizini.
lines.en: True. And I like the neighbourhood too — everything is close by.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da, ali ponekad mi smeta buka s ulice.
lines.en: Yes, but sometimes the noise from the street bothers me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Meni ne smeta. Odrastao sam u velikom gradu, navikao sam.
lines.en: It doesn't bother me. I grew up in a big city, I'm used to it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja sam odrasla u malom selu, kod bake. Tamo je bio potpuni mir.
lines.en: I grew up in a small village, at my grandma's. It was completely peaceful there.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: U kakvoj kući si živjela?
lines.en: What kind of house did you live in?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: U staroj kamenoj kući s velikim dvorištem. A ti?
lines.en: In an old stone house with a big yard. And you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja sam živio u stanu na desetom katu. Pogled je bio super, ali dvorište nismo imali.
lines.en: I lived in a flat on the tenth floor. The view was great, but we didn't have a yard.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hoćemo li jednog dana kupiti kuću s dvorištem?
lines.en: Shall we buy a house with a yard one day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rado! Ali samo ako je blizu dobrog kafića.
lines.en: Gladly! But only if it's near a good café.

78.title: Verb aspect — one idea, two verbs
78.intro: Almost every Croatian verb comes in a pair: an IMPERFECTIVE verb for ongoing, repeated or unfinished actions, and a PERFECTIVE verb for one completed event. English hides this in tenses ('was writing' vs 'wrote'); Croatian builds it into the verb itself. One key rule: perfective verbs can't describe what's happening right now — for 'I am writing' you must say Pišem, never Napišem. Learn these twelve pairs as single vocabulary items.

items.en: to write (process / completed)
items.note: Pišem mail. (I'm writing an email.) → Napisao sam mail. (I wrote it — it's done.)

items.en: to read (process / read through)
items.note: Čitam knjigu. → Pročitao sam knjigu. (I finished the book.)

items.en: to buy (habitually / once)
items.note: Kupujem namirnice svaki dan. → Jučer sam kupio vino.

items.en: to drink (process / drink up)
items.note: Pijem kavu. → Popio sam kavu i krenuo na posao.

items.en: to eat (process / eat up)
items.note: Jedem ručak. → Pojeo sam sve. (I ate everything.)

items.en: to watch (process / take a look)
items.note: Gledam film. → Pogledaj ovo! (Take a look at this!)

items.en: to study / to learn (master)
items.note: Učim hrvatski. → Naučio sam padeže. (I've learned the cases.)

items.en: to come (repeatedly) / to arrive (once)
items.note: Dolazi svaki dan. → Došao je u pet. (He arrived at five.)

items.en: to give (repeatedly) / to give (once)
items.note: Baka nam daje kolače. → Dala mi je knjigu.

items.en: to begin (be starting) / to begin (once)
items.note: Film počinje u osam. → Film je počeo prije pet minuta.

items.en: to pay (regularly) / to pay (once)
items.note: Plaćam račune svaki mjesec. → Platio sam račun i izašao.

items.en: to call (be calling) / to call up (once)
items.note: Zvao me cijelo jutro. → Nazvao me jučer navečer.

78.title: Process or completed result?
78.intro: For every action decide: is it a process, habit or duration (imperfective), or one whole completed event (perfective)?

questions.prompt: Which of these verbs is PERFECTIVE?
questions.explanation: napisati = to write and finish (one completed act). pisati, čitati and kupovati are all imperfective.

questions.prompt: Right now: 'I am writing an email.' = ?
questions.explanation: A perfective verb can't describe an action in progress — for right now you need the imperfective: Pišem.

questions.prompt: Choose the right verb and form (piti/popiti, ja): Svaki dan ______ kavu poslije ručka.
questions.explanation: svaki dan = a habit → imperfective: pijem. (popijem would stress finishing the whole cup each time — pijem is the neutral habit choice.)

questions.prompt: Jučer sam ______ cijelu knjigu. (I read the whole book.)
questions.explanation: cijelu knjigu = the whole book, completed → perfective: pročitao sam.

questions.prompt: Choose the right infinitive (učiti/naučiti): Učim hrvatski već godinu dana i želim ga dobro ______ .
questions.explanation: The goal is the completed result (to master it) → perfective: naučiti.

questions.prompt: The verb 'kupovati' is…
questions.explanation: kupovati = to be buying / buy habitually. Its perfective partner is kupiti.

questions.prompt: Ona ______ namirnice svaki dan.
questions.explanation: svaki dan = habit → imperfective present: kupuje (from kupovati).

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I wrote the email and sent it.'
questions.answer: Napisao sam mail i poslao ga
questions.explanation: Two completed events in sequence → both perfective: napisao… i poslao.

questions.prompt: What is the perfective partner of 'davati'?
questions.explanation: davati (give repeatedly) / dati (give once) — one of the highest-frequency pairs.

79.title: Aspect in the past: background vs event
79.intro: In a story, the imperfective paints the scene (what was going on) and the perfective moves the plot (what happened). 'Dok sam pisao pismo, nazvala me je sestra' — the writing is the background, the call is the event. This is the rule behind every anecdote you'll ever tell in Croatian.

items.hr: Pisao sam pismo. / Napisao sam pismo.
items.en: I was writing the letter. / I wrote (finished) the letter.
items.note: Same verb pair, completely different claim: in progress vs done.

items.hr: Dok sam pisao pismo, nazvala me je sestra.
items.en: While I was writing the letter, my sister called me.
items.note: dok + imperfective (background), perfective for the event that interrupts.

items.hr: Dok smo večerali, netko je pozvonio na vrata.
items.en: While we were having dinner, someone rang the doorbell.
items.note: večerati sets the scene; pozvoniti is the single event.

items.en: I was cooking lunch all morning.
items.note: Duration (cijelo jutro) → imperfective kuhati.

items.hr: Skuhao sam ručak i pozvao ženu za stol.
items.en: I cooked lunch (it's ready) and called my wife to the table.
items.note: kuhati/skuhati — a sequence of completed events is perfective + perfective.

items.hr: Svake subote smo išli na plac.
items.en: Every Saturday we went (used to go) to the market.
items.note: Repeated habit in the past → imperfective ići.

items.hr: Jedne subote smo otišli na izlet u Samobor.
items.en: One Saturday we went on a trip to Samobor.
items.note: One specific event → perfective otići.

items.hr: Gledao sam film kad je stigla tvoja poruka.
items.en: I was watching a film when your message arrived.
items.note: Background gledati, event stići.

items.hr: Pogledao sam film i otišao spavati.
items.en: I watched the film (to the end) and went to bed.
items.note: Both completed, one after the other → both perfective.

items.hr: Dugo sam učio hrvatski i napokon sam naučio padeže.
items.en: I studied Croatian for a long time and finally learned the cases.
items.note: The pair in one sentence: process (učio) then achieved result (naučio).

79.title: Tell it right: aspect in past narration
79.intro: Choose the aspect that matches the story: imperfective for the scene, perfective for the events.

questions.prompt: Dok sam ______ pismo, nazvala me je sestra.
questions.explanation: dok = while → background action in progress → imperfective: Dok sam pisao pismo…

questions.prompt: ______ sam pismo i poslao ga.
questions.explanation: A completed event followed by another → perfective: Napisao sam pismo i poslao ga.

questions.prompt: Choose the right participle (ići/otići, mi): Svake subote smo ______ na plac.
questions.explanation: svake subote = repeated habit → imperfective: išli smo.

questions.prompt: Choose the right participle (ići/otići, mi): Jučer smo ______ na izlet.
questions.explanation: One specific completed trip → perfective: otišli smo na izlet.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'I cooked lunch (and it's done)'?
questions.explanation: Perfective skuhati claims the result exists: lunch is ready. Kuhao sam only says I was cooking.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I was watching a film when the message arrived.'
questions.answer: Gledao sam film kad je stigla poruka
questions.explanation: Imperfective background (gledao sam) + perfective event (je stigla) — the classic narrative frame.

questions.prompt: Ona je ______ knjigu cijelo popodne.
questions.explanation: cijelo popodne = duration → imperfective: čitala je knjigu cijelo popodne.

questions.prompt: Na kraju je ______ zadnju stranicu i zatvorila knjigu.
questions.explanation: The final completed event → perfective: pročitala je zadnju stranicu.

questions.prompt: Complete (naučiti, ja m.): Učio sam hrvatski dvije godine i napokon sam ______ padeže.
questions.explanation: napokon = finally, the achieved result → perfective: naučio sam padeže.

80.title: Scaffolding your talk: Moja kvaliteta života
80.intro: Today you record a two-minute talk about how you live. Here is a complete skeleton — opener, home, habits, likes, complaints, plans, closer — that weaves together this week's locative phrases, dative constructions and aspect. Swap in your own details and the talk writes itself.

items.en: I'll tell you how I live.
items.note: A clean opener; ispričati = to tell (a story), perfective.

items.hr: Živim sa ženom u dvosobnom stanu u mirnom kvartu.
items.en: I live with my wife in a two-room flat in a quiet neighbourhood.
items.note: Two locative phrases in a row — yesterday's grammar at work.

items.hr: Prije sam živio u velikom gradu, a sada uživam u miru.
items.en: Before, I lived in a big city, and now I enjoy the peace.
items.note: uživati u + locative = to enjoy something.

items.hr: Svako jutro prošetam do pekare i popijem kavu na balkonu.
items.en: Every morning I take a walk to the bakery and drink my coffee on the balcony.
items.note: Perfective present for a complete little routine — very natural for habits seen as whole acts.

items.hr: Najviše mi se sviđa što je sve u blizini.
items.en: What I like most is that everything is close by.
items.note: sviđa mi se što… = I like (the fact) that…

items.en: Only the weekend noise bothers me.
items.note: vikendom = on weekends (instrumental of time).

items.hr: Imam jednu lošu naviku: predugo gledam mobitel.
items.en: I have one bad habit: I look at my phone too long.
items.note: predugo = for too long; honest confessions make great talks.

items.hr: Želim promijeniti tu naviku i svaki dan šetati u prirodi.
items.en: I want to change that habit and walk in nature every day.
items.note: promijeniti (perfective — one change) + šetati (imperfective — the new routine).

items.hr: Prošle godine sam počeo trčati i to je postala moja nova navika.
items.en: Last year I started running and it became my new habit.
items.note: početi + imperfective infinitive: počeo sam trčati.

items.hr: Ukratko, moj život je dobar i miran.
items.en: In short, my life is good and peaceful.
items.note: Ukratko = in short — the perfect closer for any talk.

80.title: Kvaliteta života — the integrated check
80.intro: One exercise, the whole week: listen to people describing their lives, then produce the locative phrases, dative constructions and aspect choices yourself.

questions.prompt: Listen: what bothers the speaker?
questions.explanation: Smeta mi samo buka. = Only the noise bothers me. (He loves his neighbourhood because everything is close.)
questions.audioText: Živim u malom stanu u centru grada. Najviše mi se sviđa moj kvart jer je sve blizu. Smeta mi samo buka.

questions.prompt: Listen: why did she move to Zagreb?
options: Because of the peace and quiet
questions.explanation: Tamo je bio mir, ali nije bilo posla. Sada živim u Zagrebu i imam dobar posao. = There was peace there, but no work. Now she lives in Zagreb and has a good job.
questions.audioText: Prije sam živjela u selu kod roditelja. Tamo je bio mir, ali nije bilo posla. Sada živim u Zagrebu i imam dobar posao.

questions.prompt: Listen and complete: Svako jutro ______ kavu na balkonu.
questions.explanation: Svako jutro popijem kavu na balkonu. — perfective present for a small complete routine.
questions.audioText: Svako jutro popijem kavu na balkonu.

questions.prompt: Živim u ______ . (miran kvart)
questions.explanation: Location → locative, whole phrase agrees: u mirnom kvartu.

questions.prompt: Prošle godine sam ______ trčati.
questions.explanation: One event that happened once (last year) → perfective: počeo sam trčati.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing clitic: Najviše ___ se sviđa što je sve u blizini.
questions.explanation: sviđati se + dative: Najviše mi se sviđa… (What I like most…)

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I want to change one bad habit.'
questions.explanation: promijeniti (perfective — one change) + the whole phrase in the accusative: jednu lošu naviku.

questions.prompt: Dok sam ______ u velikom gradu, nisam imao mira.
questions.explanation: dok + ongoing background state → imperfective past: Dok sam živio u velikom gradu…

questions.prompt: Complete (buka): Smeta mi ______ vikendom.
questions.explanation: With smetati the annoying thing is the subject → nominative: Smeta mi buka vikendom.
